Operation 'post-createPermit' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is too brief and lacks clarity about what permits are, what restrictions can be defined, what is returned, and the practical purpose. Added context about permit references, restriction capabilities, and return values to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Create new permits linked to a recurring contract.
data.current_description: Create permits for a recurring contract, including support for defining restrictions.
data.summary_suggestion: Create permits for recurring contract with restrictions
data.description_suggestion: Creates new permits linked to a recurring contract, allowing you to define restrictions on how the stored payment details can be used. Returns the created permit details with a unique permit reference. This enables fine-grained control over recurring payment authorization.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and lacks clarity about what permits are, what restrictions can be defined, what is returned, and the practical purpose. Added context about permit references, restriction capabilities, and return values to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'post-listRecurringDetails' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. While the description mentions recurring detail ID usage and return format, it lacks clarity about the required input (shopper reference) and the structure of the response. Enhanced version explicitly states input requirements and clarifies that a list is returned, improving AI agent ability to determine when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description:
Lists the stored payment details for a shopper, if there are any available. The recurring detail ID can be used with a regular authorisation request to charge the shopper. A summary of the payment detail is returned for presentation to the shopper.

For more information, refer to [Retrieve stored details](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-integration/recurring-payments/retrieve-stored-details/).
data.summary_suggestion: List stored payment details for shopper
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all stored payment details (tokens) for a shopper. Returns a list of recurring detail summaries including the recurring detail ID, which can be used in subsequent authorization requests to charge the shopper. Each detail includes masked payment information suitable for shopper presentation. Requires shopper reference as input.
data.reasoning: While the description mentions recurring detail ID usage and return format, it lacks clarity about the required input (shopper reference) and the structure of the response. Enhanced version explicitly states input requirements and clarifies that a list is returned, improving AI agent ability to determine when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'post-notifyShopper' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_input_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description lacks input requirements and output information. Added clarity about what inputs are needed (card details or recurring reference, amount), the regulatory context (India-specific compliance), and what is returned. This helps AI agents understand the geographic and regulatory constraints, and when to invoke this operation.

data.current_summary: Ask issuer to notify the shopper
data.current_description: Sends a request to the issuer so they can inform the shopper about the upcoming recurring payment. This endpoint is used only for local acquiring in India. For more information, refer to [Recurring card payments in India](https://docs.adyen.com/payment-methods/cards/cards-recurring-india).
data.summary_suggestion: Request issuer notification for recurring payment
data.description_suggestion: Sends a notification request to the card issuer to inform the shopper about an upcoming recurring payment. This operation is specific to local acquiring in India and is used to comply with local payment regulations. Requires the shopper's card details or recurring detail reference and payment amount information. Returns confirmation of the notification request submission.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks input requirements and output information. Added clarity about what inputs are needed (card details or recurring reference, amount), the regulatory context (India-specific compliance), and what is returned. This helps AI agents understand the geographic and regulatory constraints, and when to invoke this operation.

message: Operation 'post-scheduleAccountUpdater' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description only explains input options without clarifying the operation's purpose, what Account Updater does, or what is returned. Enhanced version explains the purpose (refresh/validate card details), clarifies both input paths, describes the side effect (card details updated with issuer), and specifies the return value. This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description:
When making the API call, you can submit either the credit card information, or the recurring detail reference and the shopper reference:
 * If the card information is provided, all the sub-fields for `card` are mandatory.
 * If the recurring detail reference is provided, the fields for `shopperReference` and `selectedRecurringDetailReference` are mandatory.
data.summary_suggestion: Schedule Account Updater for card details
data.description_suggestion: Schedules the Account Updater service to refresh stored card details. Accepts either: (1) full card information with all required sub-fields, or (2) shopper reference and recurring detail reference. The Account Updater will attempt to validate and update the card information with the issuer. Returns a result indicating whether the update was scheduled successfully. This is useful for maintaining current card details for recurring payments.
data.reasoning: Original description only explains input options without clarifying the operation's purpose, what Account Updater does, or what is returned. Enhanced version explains the purpose (refresh/validate card details), clarifies both input paths, describes the side effect (card details updated with issuer), and specifies the return value. This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation and what to expect.
